title: Institute for Climate and Sustainable Growth, University of Chicago
content: The Institute for Climate and Sustainable Growth leverages the University’s unique legacy and resources to balance the risks of a changing climate with the essential need for human progress.

title: Institute for Economic Policy Research (SIEPR), Stanford University
content: The Stanford Institute for Economic Policy Research is a nonpartisan economic research institution housed at Stanford University.

title: Doerr School of Sustainability, Stanford University
content: The Stanford Doerr School of Sustainability is a school at Stanford University focusing on climate change and sustainability. The school also researches many domains of fossil fuel extraction and development.

title: UK Wildfire 2026 heads to Leeds
content: The 2026 UK Wildfire Conference is from 10-11 November at Headingley Stadium in Leeds. The website has details on how to participate as a delegate, a speaker, a poster presenter or as a sponsor. The deadline is 24 April 2026 for speaker submissions.

title: Study links past emissions to trillions in future economic damages
content: Future climate damages from past greenhouse gas emissions dwarf the economic harm already inflicted.

title: Precipitation observing network gaps limit climate change impact assessment
content: The article shows that the global network of precipitation gauges is far too sparse to reliably monitor climate change and water risks.

title: AI tool predicts wildfire danger faster than current systems
content: A UC-led study shows AI can predict wildfire risk more accurately than existing indices, offering faster warnings and significant cost savings for fire agencies.

title: Seismic activity in California varies with the seasons
content: Earthquakes occur when the tectonic plates of the earth's crust shift. However, other natural forces can also influence seismic activity: Hydrological dynamics, like changes in groundwater and snowpacks, in particular, put pressure on faults.

title: Strengthening the Disaster Resilience of MSMEs in Barbados
content: Strengthening the Disaster Resilience of MSMEs in Barbados

title: Should we add a Category 6 to hurricane ratings?
content: An atmospheric scientist at the National Taiwan University says storms with winds above 184 miles an hour deserve their own category.

title: WHO guidance helps countries to institutionalize simulation exercises to strengthen health emergency readiness
content: To support countries prepare more systematically against emergencies, WHO has recently released new global guidance which enables countries to move beyond one-off emergency drills and establish National Health Simulation Exercise Programmes (NHSEP).

title: What the historic snow drought means for water, wildfires and the future of the West
content: Across much of the Western United States, winter 2026 was the year the snow never came. Many ski resorts got by with snowmaking but shut down their winter operations early. Fire officials and water supply managers are worried about summer.

title: Student survives 3 floods, builds flood alert app
content: Gorberg’s academic background proved critical during the floods. As an Earth sciences student in the School of Ocean and Earth Science and Technology his expertise allowed him to understand the mechanics of the disaster.

title: EU launch space-based emergency simulation in Brussels
content: The EU’s SUNSHINE project launched its first space‑based emergency simulation, integrating major EU space systems to train responders through wildfire and flood scenarios, strengthen coordination, and improve crisis decision‑making across Member States.

title: Connecting climate science to local adaptation action
content: Higher resolution climate data is now part of the Met Office Local Authority Climate Service (LACS) following a notable update in response to user feedback.

title: Why emotional resilience should be at the heart of climate change education
content: The article argues that climate education must include emotional resilience, as young people feel worry, powerlessness and disengagement. Supporting wellbeing, agency and meaningful action can better prepare them for a climate‑changed future.

title: Warming coastal waters primary driver of large-scale humid heatwaves
content: Rising sea surface temperatures in coastal waters are driving 50 to 64 percent of the increase in large-scale humid heatwaves according to new research.

title: Niue marks World Meteorological Day with launch of children’s disaster booklets
content: Niue marked World Meteorological Day by launching Vagahau Niue COPE disaster-preparedness booklets to empower children and strengthen community resilience.

title: Pacific Meteorological Desk Partnership
content: The Pacific Meteorological Desk Partnership (PMDP) is a regional coordinated response to meeting weather and climate services development in the Pacific Islands region.

title: Climate change adaptation laws and policies: A review of trends, gaps and opportunities in 35 countries
content: This policy report, published by the Grantham Research Institute on Climate Change and the Environment, outlines an overview of the climate adaptation strategies of 35 selected countries.

title: Using ‘imaginative’ AI to survey past and future earthquake damage
content: Visualizing ground-level damage offers insight into next disaster, study finds.

title: Australia must brace for clusters of disasters, not just isolated fires and floods
content: Australia faces rising clusters of overlapping disasters as climate change intensifies heat, storms, fires and floods. The article urges stronger planning, clearer public messaging and community involvement to manage simultaneous hazards.

title: Strengthening disaster resilience of SMEs through business continuity planning – Guidance and Templates
content: Strengthening disaster resilience of SMEs through business continuity planning – Guidance and Templates

title: When Tokyo’s next big earthquake hits, could global markets crash?
content: A major Tokyo earthquake could trigger huge uninsured losses, forcing banks to sell foreign assets and potentially shaking global markets. The city’s wealth concentration and seismic risk create a rare but serious financial vulnerability.

title: OpenPlanet
content: OpenPlanet wishes to democratize climate adaptation by providing high-fidelity, no-code climate risk intelligence.

title: Climate change is altering Saharan dust – and Europe is downwind
content: Climate change is reshaping Saharan dust patterns, increasing risks for Europe through poorer air quality, faster snowmelt and wider environmental impacts, prompting calls for stronger prevention and cross‑border cooperation.

title: Heatwaves will be worst for rural parts of Africa – new model shows tens of millions face dangerous warming by 2100
content: Rural Africans will face far greater heatwave exposure than urban residents by 2100, as climate change and population growth intensify risks. The study urges urgent emissions cuts and investment in rural heat protection and health systems.

title: Nature-based Solutions for landslide risk reduction in informal settlements of Salvador da Bahia, Brazil
content: Study finds nature‑based solutions can match or exceed traditional engineering for landslide risk reduction in Salvador’s informal settlements, at lower cost and with added social and ecological benefits.

title: Structural safety and widlfires
content: Structural safety and widlfires

title: From dialogue to delivery: how the EU–LAC MoU is turning political commitment into coordinated action on disaster risk management
content: From the outset, the MoU was designed to be practical: an innovative framework that supports regular engagement, facilitates effective identification of shared priorities, and creates a common platform for technical cooperation and knowledge exchange.

title: A practical training manual to help communities deliver nature-based solutions for urban resilience in the Congo basin
content: This manual is designed to train communities and local governments, making these practices accessible, scalable, and adaptable to local contexts.

title: Machine learning could transform how infrastructure recovers from natural hazards
content: A review published in Civil Engineering Sciences examines how machine learning (ML) methods are being applied to support infrastructure recovery following natural hazards.

title: Civil Engineering Sciences (AAAS)
content: Civil Engineering Sciences is a peer-reviewed, interdisciplinary journal focusing on the latest progress of fundamental science and engineering science in this field.

title: See NASA’s GUARDIAN catch a tsunami
content: A data visualization technique illustrates how an experimental NASA technology can provide extra lead time to communities in the path of a tsunami.

title: The Resilience Insight Africa knowledge hub launched to strengthen risk-informed urban development
content: An online knowledge hub is now available to support African cities in responding to climate risks, disasters, and rapid urban growth.

title: AI model could revolutionize flood forecasting
content: A flood forecasting model developed at the University of Minnesota combines elements of the traditional physics-based models with newer machine-learning techniques.

title: Decision making under pressure: new skills for the new normal
content: The Australian Institute for Disaster Resilience masterclass focuses on improving decision-making in disaster contexts by addressing uncertainty, cognitive biases, team dynamics, and psychological safety in high-pressure environments.

title: Climate change may complicate avalanche risk across the Pacific Northwest
content: Warming temperatures throughout the Pacific Northwest are likely to complicate avalanche forecasting in the coming years, according to a new UW study.

title: Building resilience into the grid: resilience metrics and standards for transmission systems
content: This report outlines metrics and standards to assess, quantify, and enhance the resilience of transmission systems, building on previous work on power sector resilience.

title: Satellite driven model provides “more realistic and reliable” predictions of sand and dust storm emissions
content: The technology used to predict sand and dust storm (SDS) severity has for decades systematically over-estimated when and where sediment is transported across the Earth’s surface, a new study shows.
